rationale: Option B's grounding is exact: bead.WorktreeList() at bdcli.go:202-217 runs `bd worktree list --json`; defaultWorktreeOps.List() (mindspec_executor.go:44-46) returns bead.WorktreeList(), the SAME source FinalizeEpic consumes at :383 (HasPrefix :385, IsAncestor :394, MergeInto :402, loop :381-422) — the same-source merge-prevention claim holds. internal/approve already imports internal/bead (impl.go:13, no new edge); the implXxxFn seam convention pre-exists; no code diff vs origin/main, so internal/bead is consumed read-only and FindOrphanedClosedBeads (orphans.go:74) is byte-unchanged. Commit e239c4e5 touches spec.md only (16+/15-). Both round-7 test names have zero Go-code hits — proofs genuinely RED. BranchExists gitops.go:94-100, BeadBranchPrefix worktree.go:21, MergeBase impl.go:249, IsAncestor gitops.go:446-463 all exact. ONE tree-unsupported claim: AC13 and Validation Proofs assert the test name has 'ZERO hits repo-wide at bcb255eb', but TestApproveImpl_WorktreeEnumRefusesDespiteProbeMiss appears at bcb255eb in reviews/spec-115-toctou-decision/DECISION-OUTCOME.md:13 — the commit that coined the name. The round-6 names at 18cc59d9 were truly zero; this one is not. AC10's 'ZERO code hits' phrasing is the accurate template. Narrow wording fix; falsifiability unaffected.
concrete_changes_required: AC13 and the Validation Proofs bullet: replace the false 'the test name has ZERO hits repo-wide at bcb255eb' claim for TestApproveImpl_WorktreeEnumRefusesDespiteProbeMiss with the accurate scope AC10 already uses — e.g. 'ZERO hits in Go code at bcb255eb (the name is coined in the round-7 disposition artifact reviews/spec-115-toctou-decision/DECISION-OUTCOME.md:13, which cannot satisfy the grep -q func-existence discriminator)'. TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant is genuinely zero repo-wide at bcb255eb and needs no change.

findings.item: Worktree-enum leg grounding (bead.WorktreeList bdcli.go:202-217; same source as FinalizeEpic via defaultWorktreeOps.List :44-46 -> :383; approve imports bead impl.go:13, no new import edge; no internal/bead change lands)
findings.note: All cites verified exact against 50165d81; empty code diff vs origin/main confirms read-only consumption.

findings.item: Leg added-to (not replacing) probe detection; shared predicate byte-unchanged for complete/next/doctor; fail-closed on WorktreeList/ancestry error; epic-scoped
findings.note: R1/Scope/OQ2 text consistent; orphans.go byte-identical to main (no code changes at all on the branch); deliberate asymmetry vs FinalizeEpic's own skip-on-error at :383/:394-395 is accurately described.

findings.item: Round-6 TOCTOU closed by same-source enumeration
findings.note: Because the gate keys off bd worktree list — the identical production source behind WorktreeOps.List() — a transient BranchExists miss cannot desync scan from merge. Mechanism verified in code; Option B 4/5 confirmed in DECISION-OUTCOME.md.

findings.item: Honest residual: Fact 2 re-scoped to PERSISTENTLY masked ref; three residuals disclosed; two-List() window bounded by serialized-lifecycle model; no 'implausible' claim
findings.note: Goal names all three residuals; Non-Goals discloses the two-call window and the future single-snapshot hardening without claiming total closure.

Test absent from Go code (proof chain genuinely RED; revert-to-probe-only subtest, fail-closed subtest, no-false-positive subtests all well-formed), but the 'ZERO hits repo-wide at bcb255eb' parenthetical is false — one hit in DECISION-OUTCOME.md:13 at that SHA. Wording fix only.

findings.item: AC10 RED-on-revert ownership test replacing the pre-applied grep
findings.note: TestLifecycleOwnershipExactlyOneWorkflowClaimant has zero code hits (and zero repo-wide hits at bcb255eb); 'ZERO code hits' phrasing accurate; exactly-one-workflow invariant falsifiable.

findings.item: Diff scope: e239c4e5 is spec.md only
findings.note: git show confirms 1 file, 16+/15-. The 8b07e89f..e239c4e5 RANGE additionally contains bcb255eb (toctou-decision panel artifacts under reviews/ — process records, no code or spec surface).
